opportunities.title: Pathfinder Cuts Ties With Archives of Nethys: Where Do the Rules Actually Live Now?
opportunities.why_now: AoN has been the de facto official PF2e rules reference for years, and this split forces every table to figure out a new source of truth overnight.
opportunities.angle: News breakdown + practical guide ranking alternative rules references (Pathbuilder, Nethys mirrors, print SRD) for accuracy and completeness

opportunities.title: The ORC License Was Supposed to Prevent Exactly This — So What Happened?
opportunities.why_now: The AoN/Paizo split is the first real stress-test of promises made during the OGL fallout, and rules-focused readers want to know if 'open' licensing actually held up.
opportunities.angle: Legal/rules deep-dive comparing ORC license terms to what's unfolding, written in a myth-busting 'here's what the text actually says' style

opportunities.title: This Week's Crowdfunding Slate, Ranked by Actual Rules Innovation
opportunities.why_now: Deathbringer, Sly Flourish, and Gary's Appendix all launched/updated crowdfunding pushes in the same news cycle, giving a clean comparison hook.
opportunities.angle: Backer's scorecard format grading each project on new mechanics vs. reskinned content, with a clear pledge/pass verdict

opportunities.title: $500 for Deadfall: Auditing What Beadle & Grimm's Premium Box Actually Adds to the Rules
opportunities.why_now: A $500 price point for a D&D boxed set is inherently clickable, and the rules-first angle is separating genuine new content from collector packaging.
opportunities.angle: Line-item breakdown of bonus adventures/subclasses vs. minis/posters, framed as 'here's what you're really paying for'

opportunities.title: Gnomecast Talks 'Seeking Good Players' — But the Real Rule Is You Need a Written Player Contract
opportunities.why_now: Gnome Stew's fresh episode on player vetting is a jumping-off point for a rules-first take on codifying table expectations instead of leaving them unwritten.
opportunities.angle: Reactive essay turning a soft-skills podcast topic into a hard, rules-first argument: 'if it's not written down, it's not a rule'
